keynotes.title: Decision model for durable clinical benefit from front- or late-line immunotherapy alone or with chemotherapy in large population of non-small cell lung cancer

keynote.title: Decision model for durable clinical benefit from front- or late-line immunotherapy alone or with chemotherapy in large population of non-small cell lung cancer
keynote.core_problem: Predictive biomarkers and models of immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) have been extensively studied in NSCLC, but evidence for many biomarkers remains inconclusive, and the opaqueness of machine learning models hinders their practicality.
contributions: Provided compelling evidence for biomarkers predictive of durable clinical benefit (DCB) in NSCLC patients treated with ICIs.
Developed a transparent and clinically relevant decision-tree model (DT10) that outperforms other models and FDA-approved biomarkers in predicting DCB.
methods: Consolidated data from ICI-treated patients with NSCLC (n = 3,288) across real-world multicentre studies, public cohorts, and the Choice-01 trial (NCT03607539).
Gathered over 50 features to examine the predictive capacity of DCB from mono-ICIs and ICIs plus chemotherapy.
Used multivariate logistic regression analysis to identify significant factors and mutation subtypes associated with DCB.
Constructed a decision tree model (DT10) using identified clinicopathological markers and genomic mutations.
Explored the tumour microenvironment and peripheral PD-1+ CD8+ T-cell receptor profiles.
experiments_or_evidence: Multivariate logistic regression analysis identified tumour histology, PD-L1 expression, tumour mutational burden, line, and regimen of ICI treatment as significant factors.
Mutation subtypes of EGFR, KRAS, KEAP1, STK11, and disruptive TP53 mutations demonstrated significant association with DCB.

The DT10 model predicted DCB with superior performance in the training set (N = 286, AUC = 0.82) and consistently outperformed other models and FDA-approved biomarkers in internal and external test sets.
DT10 predicted-DCB patients had significantly longer progression-free survival and overall survival than -NDB patients (HR < 1, p < 0.05).

DT10 predicted-DCB group presented an enriched inflamed tumour immune phenotype (67%) and higher peripheral TCR diversity, whereas the -NDB group showed an enriched desert immune phenotype (86%) and higher peripheral TCR clonality.
assumptions: The selected biomarkers and clinical features are representative of the broader NSCLC patient population.
limitations: The study is based on retrospective data, which may introduce biases.
The DT10 model's performance may vary in different patient populations or when new treatments are introduced.

gap_signals.text: Exploring additional biomarkers and integrating more diverse datasets could enhance the model's predictive power.
The DT10 model's performance may vary in different patient populations or when new treatments are introduced.
The study is based on retrospective data, which may introduce biases.
Further validation in prospective studies is needed to confirm the generalizability of the DT10 model.

datasets_or_materials: Data from ICI-treated patients with NSCLC (n = 3,288) across real-world multicentre studies, public cohorts, and the Choice-01 trial (NCT03607539).

important_claims.evidence: The DT10 model achieved an AUC of 0.82 in the training set and consistently outperformed logistic regression, random forest, support vector machine models, and FDA-approved biomarkers of PD-L1 and TMB in internal and external test sets.

important_claims.claim: The DT10 model is interpretable and visually engaging, making it clinically relevant.

keynotes.title: Predictive Modeling of Survival and Toxicity in Patients With Hepatocellular Carcinoma After Radiotherapy

keynote.title: Predictive Modeling of Survival and Toxicity in Patients With Hepatocellular Carcinoma After Radiotherapy
keynote.core_problem: To stratify patients and aid clinical decision making by predicting treatment failure and radiation-induced toxicities after radiotherapy (RT) in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) across institutions.
contributions: Developed machine learning models to predict survival, nonlocal failure, radiation-induced liver disease, and lymphopenia from baseline patient and treatment parameters.
Models were validated externally using an independent cohort, demonstrating the potential for reliable outcome predictions and novel strategies for patient stratification and treatment selection.
methods: Used linear and nonlinear algorithms, including logistic regression, support vector machine, multilayer perceptron, and gradient boosted trees.
Trained models on 207 patients from Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH) and validated them on 143 patients from MD Anderson Cancer Center (MDACC).
Optimized model structures using a nested cross-validation approach to prevent overfitting.
Preregistered methodology in a Study Analysis Plan before obtaining access to the validation data set to prevent selective reporting and positive bias.
experiments_or_evidence: The survival model stratified high-risk versus low-risk patients well in the external validation cohort (c-index = 0.75), better than existing risk scores.
Predictions of 1-year survival and nonlocal failure were excellent (external AUC = 0.74 and 0.80, respectively), especially in the high-risk group (accuracy > 90%).
Predictions of liver disease and lymphopenia were good but less robust (external AUC = 0.68 and 0.7, respectively).
assumptions: Baseline patient and treatment parameters are sufficient for predicting outcomes.
Nested cross-validation effectively prevents overfitting.
External validation on an independent cohort is a valid method for assessing model performance.
limitations: Predictions of liver disease and lymphopenia were less robust, suggesting the need for more comprehensive consideration of dosimetry and better predictive biomarkers.
Differences in patient characteristics between the training and validation cohorts may affect model generalizability.

gap_signals.text: Predictions of liver disease and lymphopenia were less robust, suggesting the need for more comprehensive consideration of dosimetry and better predictive biomarkers.
Differences in patient characteristics between the training and validation cohorts may affect model generalizability.

important_claims.evidence: The survival model stratified high-risk versus low-risk patients well in the external validation cohort (c-index = 0.75), better than existing risk scores. Predictions of 1-year survival and nonlocal failure were excellent (external AUC = 0.74 and 0.80, respectively), especially in the high-risk group (accuracy > 90%).

important_claims.claim: The excellent performance, particularly in high-risk patients, suggests novel strategies for patient stratification and treatment selection.
important_claims.evidence: Cause-of-death analysis showed differential modes of treatment failure in these cohorts and indicated that these models could be used to stratify RT patients for liver-sparing treatment regimen or combination approaches with systemic agents.

keynotes.title: Predictors and effects of toxicity experienced by older adults with cancer receiving systemic therapy in a randomized clinical trial of geriatric assessment.

keynote.title: Predictors and effects of toxicity experienced by older adults with cancer receiving systemic therapy in a randomized clinical trial of geriatric assessment
keynote.core_problem: Older adults with cancer are underrepresented in clinical research, and the implications of treatment toxicity can be greater for this population. There is a need to predict toxicity and its effects on quality of life and functional status.
contributions: Analyzed data from a clinical trial (5C study) to assess predictors of grade 3-5 toxicity in older adults with cancer.
Evaluated the impact of toxicity on quality of life and functional decline.
methods: Used logistic regression and pattern mixture models to assess if baseline CARG toxicity score, G8 score, and ECOG performance predicted grade 3-5 toxicity.
Assessed the impact of toxicity on quality of life and functional decline over six months.
experiments_or_evidence: Data from 316 patients (mean age 76 years, 40% female) were analyzed, with 38% experiencing at least one grade 3-5 toxicity.
Neither the CARG toxicity score, G8, nor ECOG were predictive of grade 3-5 toxicity.
Patients who experienced grade 3-5 toxicity were more likely to have functional impairments over time (odds ratio 3.71, p = 0.03), but maintained their quality of life.
assumptions: The baseline scores (CARG, G8, ECOG) might be predictive of grade 3-5 toxicity in older adults with cancer.
Toxicity may impact quality of life and functional status in older adults with cancer.
limitations: No predictors of grade 3-5 toxicity were found among the baseline scores used in the study.

gap_signals.text: No predictors of grade 3-5 toxicity were found among the baseline scores used in the study.
Further research is needed to identify predictors of grade 3-5 toxicity in older adults with cancer.

datasets_or_materials: Data from the 5C study (NCT0315467).

important_claims.claim: Neither the CARG toxicity score, G8, or ECOG were predictive of grade 3-5 toxicity.
important_claims.evidence: Logistic regression and pattern mixture model analysis of 316 patients showed no significant prediction of grade 3-5 toxicity by these scores.

important_claims.claim: Patients who experienced grade 3-5 toxicity were more likely to have functional impairments over time.
important_claims.evidence: Odds ratio 3.71, p = 0.03, indicating a statistically significant association between toxicity and functional decline.

steps.claim: Patients who experience grade 3-5 toxicity are more likely to have functional impairments over time.
steps.evidence: Odds ratio 3.71, p = 0.03.

causal_chains.outcome: Functional decline over time

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study highlights the need for further research to identify predictors of severe toxicity in older adults with cancer and suggests that increased support may benefit those who experience toxicity, as it is associated with functional decline.

keynotes.title: Prediction of PLX-4720 Sensitivity in Cancer Cell Lines through Multi-Omics Integration and Attention-Based Fusion Modeling

keynote.title: Prediction of PLX-4720 Sensitivity in Cancer Cell Lines through Multi-Omics Integration and Attention-Based Fusion Modeling
keynote.core_problem: Predicting the sensitivity of cancer cell lines to PLX-4720, a preclinical BRAF inhibitor, requires models capable of capturing the multilayered regulation of oncogenic signaling. Single-omics predictors are often insufficient because drug response is shaped by interactions among multiple molecular layers.
contributions: Development of an attention-based multi-omics integration framework for predicting PLX-4720 sensitivity.
Highlighting the importance of modality selection over maximal data depth in multi-omics integration.
methods: Use of genomic, epigenomic, transcriptomic, proteomic, metabolomic, and protein interaction (PPI) data from the GDSC1 panel.
Encoding each modality into a latent representation using feed-forward neural networks or graph convolutional networks.
Fusion of modalities through an attention mechanism that assigns modality-specific importance weights.
Regression model used to predict PLX-4720 response.
experiments_or_evidence: Validation R² values above 0.92 for the best two-modality combination (genomics + transcriptomics).
Epigenomics as the strongest single-omics predictor.
Integration of three to five omics layers improves stability but does not surpass the accuracy of the best two-modality combinations.
assumptions: Drug response is influenced by interactions among genomic alterations, epigenetic regulation, transcriptional activity, protein signaling, metabolic state, and network-level context.
limitations: Small sample size (18–28 cell lines per configuration), making models susceptible to variability.
Dataset consists of baseline static molecular profiles, without post-treatment dynamics.

gap_signals.text: Dataset consists of baseline static molecular profiles, without post-treatment dynamics.
Small sample size (18–28 cell lines per configuration), making models susceptible to variability.
Incorporating drug chemical structure, leveraging transfer learning or multitask learning, integrating pathway-level or network module information.

datasets_or_materials: Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC) database

important_claims.claim: The best performance is achieved by integrating genomics and transcriptomics, which yields validation R² values above 0.92.
important_claims.evidence: Across single- and multi-omics configurations, the best performance is achieved by integrating genomics and transcriptomics, which yields validation R2 values above 0.92.

important_claims.claim: Epigenomics is the strongest single-omics predictor, while metabolomics and PPI data contribute additional context when combined with other modalities.
important_claims.evidence: Epigenomics is the strongest single-omics predictor, while metabolomics and PPI data contribute additional context when combined with other modalities.

steps.claim: Mutational status and downstream transcriptional activation shape sensitivity to BRAF inhibition.
steps.evidence: This reflects the complementary roles of mutational status and downstream transcriptional activation in shaping sensitivity to BRAF inhibition.

causal_chains.outcome: Achieves validation R² values above 0.92

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The proposed framework provides an efficient and biologically grounded strategy for drug response prediction and supports the development of precision pharmacogenomics. It highlights the importance of modality selection rather than maximal data depth, which can be applied to other similar studies.

sub_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If PK parameters, toxicity, or dosing do not vary with genetic variants and molecular phenotypes in a consistent and reproducible manner.

objective_decomposition.objective: 在定义明确的患者群体、疾病类型和治疗类别中，个体的遗传变异、肿瘤或疾病分子表型、器官功能、免疫状态、既往治疗史以及制造过程参数，是否能够以可重复、可校准、因果上可解释的方式预测：1. 某一治疗方案的药代动力学暴露、毒性和适宜剂量；2. 某一靶向药物、免疫治疗、细胞治疗或核酸药物的临床获益与耐药风险；3. 个体化制剂或自体细胞产品能否在身份、纯度、效力、无菌性、批间一致性、周转时间和成本约束下稳定制造；4. 上述预测在不同祖源、年龄、肝肾功能、疾病分期、医疗中心和检测平台迁移时是否仍成立。禁止将统计相关、患者画像匹配或模型准确率直接解释为因果治疗获益；禁止将实验室样本、类器官、动物模型或单中心回顾性结果直接外推为临床个体化治疗有效；禁止将技术尚不成熟误判为生物学上绝对不可行。执行完整闭环：Boxue → Objective Decomposition → ZhiZhi → TanXi → Socrates → MingLi → YanZhen → DuZhi → BianLun → MingLi 修订 → YanZhen 复审。
objective_decomposition.domain: Personalized Medicine / Precision Medicine / Pharmacogenomics / Cancer Genomics / Clinical Pharmacology / Multi-omics / Patient-derived Models / Cell and Gene Therapy Manufacturing / Biostatistics / Regulatory Science
objective_decomposition.research_brief:
创建并运行一个 AutoGen 科研闭环验证项目。

  项目标题：
  Can we design and manufacture medicines customized for individual people?
  从个体分子表型、剂量响应到可制造性的精准医疗因果证据闭环验证

  领域：
  Personalized Medicine / Precision Medicine / Pharmacogenomics / Cancer Genomics /
  Clinical Pharmacology / Multi-omics / Patient-derived Models /
  Cell and Gene Therapy Manufacturing / Biostatistics / Regulatory Science

  研究目标：
  不要泛泛讨论 精准医疗将改变未来医疗。围绕以下可分解、可证伪的科学与转化问题开展验证：

  在定义明确的患者群体、疾病类型和治疗类别中，个体的遗传变异、肿瘤或疾病分子表型、
  器官功能、免疫状态、既往治疗史以及制造过程参数，是否能够以可重复、可校准、
  因果上可解释的方式预测：

  1. 某一治疗方案的药代动力学暴露、毒性和适宜剂量；
  2. 某一靶向药物、免疫治疗、细胞治疗或核酸药物的临床获益与耐药风险；
  3. 个体化制剂或自体细胞产品能否在身份、纯度、效力、无菌性、批间一致性、
     周转时间和成本约束下稳定制造；
  4. 上述预测在不同祖源、年龄、肝肾功能、疾病分期、医疗中心和检测平台迁移时是否仍成立。

  禁止将统计相关、患者画像匹配或模型准确率直接解释为因果治疗获益；
  禁止将实验室样本、类器官、动物模型或单中心回顾性结果直接外推为临床个体化治疗有效；
  禁止将技术尚不成熟误判为生物学上绝对不可行。

  执行完整闭环：
  Boxue → Objective Decomposition → ZhiZhi → TanXi → Socrates →
  MingLi → YanZhen → DuZhi → BianLun → MingLi 修订 → YanZhen 复审。


  ## Boxue：Objective Decomposition

  在任何宽泛检索前，先运行 decompose_research_objective。
  输出不是一组研究方向，而是独立的、可被推翻的子假设。

 

  ## ZhiZhi：文献与证据知识库要求

  数据源与质量边界

  - 主要使用 semantic_scholar、PubMed、arXiv；临床前沿预印本可补充 medRxiv/bioRxiv，但必须明确其未经同行评审状态。
  - 分层导入至少 15 篇经质量筛选的文献；数量不是目标，必须覆盖每个已选子假设的证据窗口。
  - 对摘要缺失、unknown 字段、来源可疑或 requires_human_review 的记录，执行提取修复、降权或停留在背景层，不得作为机制合
    同的唯一证据。

  - 不得制造论文、临床试验、监管文件、效应量、患者结局或指南推荐。

  P0–P4 分层

  P0 / L3：近期预印本
    - 精准医疗、临床药理、多组学、患者来源模型、个体化细胞/核酸制造
    - 要求标注未经同行评审；检索失败必须显式标记证据不足

  P1 / L2：近2年高质量原始研究
    - 前瞻性临床队列、随机或准实验研究
    - 外部验证、临床药理、真实世界目标试验模拟
    - 多中心临床/转化研究和受控制造研究

  P2 / L4：机制、实验与理论证据
    - 酶动力学、PK/PD、药物转运、受体占有
    - 单细胞、多组学、功能测定、类器官
    - CMC、效力测定、放行标准、过程分析技术

  P3 / L0：系统综述、临床指南、监管和方法学综述
    - 药物基因组学实施指南
    - 精准肿瘤学、患者来源模型和先进治疗产品监管综述
    - 偏倚、因果推断、临床预测模型报告规范

  P4 / L1：奠基研究与历史脉络
    - 关键药物基因组学机制发现
    - 个体化靶向治疗或细胞治疗的奠基性临床/制造研究
    - 仅用于历史和机制脉络，不得替代当前外部验证

  必须覆盖的子空间

  - 药物基因组学、代谢酶、转运体、HLA 风险、PK/PD 与剂量优化；
  - 肿瘤或疾病基因组、转录组、蛋白组、单细胞和空间组学；
  - 预测性标志物与预后性标志物的因果区分；
  - 真实世界数据、目标试验模拟、选择偏倚、祖源偏倚和检测平台漂移；
  - 患者来源类器官、PDX、功能测定与临床结局之间的迁移误差；
  - 自体细胞、核酸药物或个体化制剂的 CMC、身份链、效力、无菌、放行和供应链；
  - 监管、知情同意、数据治理、隐私、算法审计和可及性；
  - 经济性：周转时间、失败制造批次、支付方式和中心间可复制性。

  必须排除或降权的内容

  - 只报告模型 AUC、没有临床终点或外部验证的纯算法论文；
  - 只有疾病关联、没有治疗策略或可观测中介的关联研究；
  - 与精准医疗无关的普通生物信息学、一般医院调度或常规药物合成论文；
  - 仅动物或细胞系研究、却声称直接预测患者获益的论文；
  - 没有来源、无明确样本定义、混合多个治疗场景且不可拆分的营销报告。

  DeepSurvey 风格知识构建

  每篇入库论文除 method-scenario-benchmark 外，必须提取：

  keynote:
    core_problem: 该论文解决的具体临床或制造问题
    patient_population: 人群、适应证、入排标准和治疗场景
    causal_chains:
      - trigger: 干预、遗传变异、制造参数或自然实验条件
        steps:
          - claim: 中介机制
            evidence: 论文中的来源摘录
            evidence_type: experimental | theoretical | observational | inferred
        outcome: 临床、药代、功能或制造终点
        observables: 可直接测量的信号
        interventions: 可操纵变量
    experimental_evidence: 样本数、测量条件、对照和主要终点
    assumptions: 隐含假设、可迁移条件和未测混杂
    limitations: 作者明确局限
    important_claims: 声明与证据的一一对应
    future_directions: 明确未来工作
    code_or_implementation: 公开代码、模型版本、分析环境；不得下载或处理患者敏感数据

  ## TanXi：Gap 探索要求

  TanXi 首先做因果断链检测，而不是把方法尚未用于某场景作为高价值 Gap。

  优先发现以下问题：

  1. 遗传变异 → 功能表型 → 药物暴露 → 毒性/获益 中，哪一段缺乏可测量中介、干预证据或外部验证；
  2. 某些生物标志物究竟预测治疗效应，还是仅反映预后、疾病负荷或医生选择治疗的偏倚；
  3. 单次基线多组学预测与治疗过程中克隆演化、耐药、免疫状态变化之间的断链；
  4. 类器官、PDX 或功能检测的体外响应与临床响应不一致的机制：采样偏倚、微环境缺失、时间滞后还是剂量暴露差异；
  5. 个体化细胞/核酸产品中，起始材料、过程参数、关键质量属性、效力和临床可给药性之间是否存在未验证的因果链；
  6. 不同祖源、年龄、肝肾功能、合并用药、检测平台和医疗中心之间，预测规则是否出现校准失效；
  7. 预测性能改进是否被制造周期、失败率、成本和可及性抵消；
  8. 监管/标准体系是否无法覆盖个体化产品的身份链、效力检测和持续工艺变更。

  每个 ranked gap 必须输出：

  mechanism_draft:
    input: 已定义的干预、患者分层或制造变量
    proposed_mediator: 仅可填写文献来源支持的中介；否则保持 unresolved
    output: 明确临床、药代、功能或制造终点
    source_clue: 原文 citation 与 excerpt
    causal_gap:
      missing_kind: intermediate_mechanism | observability | intervention |
                    counterfactual | evidence_window
      evidence_needed: 下一步需要何种直接证据
    sub_hypothesis_id: SH1 | SH2 | SH3 | SH4
    unresolved_fields:
      - identity
      - location_or_scope
      - dynamics
      - reversibility
      - observability
      - intervention
      - counterfactual

  禁止把某 AI 方法尚未用于某癌种某测序技术还没和某药物配对直接列为核心 Gap。

  ## Socrates：机制合同与因果补全

  只选择排名第一、且属于机制型或矛盾型的 Gap 建立机制合同。

  Socrates evidence contract:
    identity: 中介实体/过程到底是什么
    location_or_scope: 位于哪类细胞、组织、患者分层、制造环节或时间窗
    dynamics: 如何随剂量、治疗周期、病程或制造批次变化
    reversibility: 是否可逆，何种条件下可恢复
    observability: 如何直接测量，而不是只测临床终点
    intervention: 什么变量可在伦理和技术上被操纵
    counterfactual: 若抑制/移除中介，预期发生什么

  每个字段必须有：

  evidence:
    - citation: 真实论文、试验、指南或受控制造来源
      excerpt: 支持该字段的原文摘录
      paper_id: PaperGraph记录
      evidence_type: experimental | theoretical | observational | inferred

  Socrates 必须同时输出：

  - 反事实或准实验设计；
  - 第一性原理推导待补项，例如酶动力学、药物暴露—反应关系、受体占有、细胞扩增动力学、质量属性—效力关系；
  - 竞争机制消除计划；
  - 哪些证据来自人体、体外系统、动物模型或制造过程，禁止混淆证据层级。

  若 verdict != COMPLETE，输出 INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE、剩余字段和定向检索建议，停止 MingLi 假设生成；不得用常识、临床直觉
  或模板补全。

  ## MingLi：假设生成要求

  MingLi 只可消费：

  - Socrates COMPLETE 的机制合同；
  - 对应子假设满足 P0–P2 证据窗口；
  - 有明确患者范围、治疗范围和可观测终点的 Gap。

  每条假设必须包含：

  hypothesis:
    sub_hypothesis_id: SHx
    patient_scope: 年龄、适应证、疾病阶段、关键入排条件
    treatment_scope: 具体药物/治疗类别/制造平台
    causal_chain: X → Z → Y
    X: 可干预变量或可定义的自然实验
    Z: 直接可测量中介
    Y: 临床、药代、功能或制造终点
    operating_conditions:
      - 给药方案、剂量、治疗周期
      - 肝肾功能、合并用药、样本采集时点
      - 制造工艺、放行窗口或储运条件
    observables:
      - 药物暴露、毒性分级、反应率、无进展生存
      - ctDNA、转录组、蛋白组、免疫功能、类器官功能读数
      - 身份、纯度、效力、无菌、周转时间和失败率
    null_hypothesis: 不存在经中介 Z 传递的治疗效应异质性
    alternative_explanations:
      - 未测临床混杂
      - 预后性而非预测性标志物
      - 检测误差、选择偏倚或时间依赖偏倚
      - 制造批次或中心效应
    falsification_condition: 预注册的失败条件
    source_mapping: 每个因果链步骤对应 Socrates citation/excerpt

  合格假设的形式应类似：

  > 在预先定义的患者分层和治疗方案中，若通过 X 改变或识别个体化治疗条件，并且直接测得中介 Z 的预期变化，而 Y 在匹配对照
  > 下按预注册方向改变，则支持该机制；若 Z 未改变、Y 与 Z 脱钩，或竞争机制更能解释数据，则否定或修订假设。

  禁止使用AI 能改善个体化治疗通过多组学理解患者采用特定技术提升疗效之类没有机制实体和证伪条件的万能模板。

  ## YanZhen：两次机制审计

  初审

  - 内部一致性：变量、患者范围、治疗范围、时间顺序和中介定义是否一致；
  - 数据一致性：citation/excerpt 是否真正支持所声称的人群、干预和终点；
  - 因果一致性：是否把相关误当治疗效应，是否遗漏关键混杂；
  - 工程与生物学区分：制造失败、成本或周转时间限制不能被误判为疗法生物学无效；
  - 输出：unsupported_claims、required_actions、verdict。

  至少两个 regime-shift 检验

  regime_shift_1:
    condition: 祖源、年龄或性别构成发生变化
    question: 遗传风险或治疗预测是否仍校准，是否存在群体公平性失效

  regime_shift_2:
    condition: 肝肾功能、合并用药、疾病分期或治疗线数变化
    question: 剂量—暴露—毒性因果链是否仍成立

  regime_shift_3:
    condition: 检测平台、样本采集时间点、医疗中心或制造设施变化
    question: 生物标志物和质量属性是否可重复，中心效应是否主导结论

  regime_shift_4:
    condition: 从回顾性队列迁移到前瞻性、真实世界或受控临床研究
    question: 是否存在标签泄漏、选择偏倚、时间依赖偏倚或疗效衰减

  若初审不是 PASS，YanZhen 必须把具体失败点反馈给 MingLi 和 DuZhi；不得直接输出成功结论。

  ## DuZhi + BianLun：3–5 轮结构化辩论

  DuZhi 每轮必须针对上一轮的具体回应追问，并至少覆盖：

  1. 个体化的操作定义是什么：是药物选择、剂量、给药时机、组合疗法，还是产品制造参数？
  2. 遗传或多组学标志物如何从关联提升为预测性治疗标志物？是否存在治疗分配偏倚？
  3. 中介变量 Z 如何直接测量？检测误差、批次效应和采样时间是否足以掩盖真实变化？
  4. 若不同中心、不同祖源或不同检测平台中预测效果显著下降，机制是否仍成立？
  5. 若患者来源模型预测有效但临床无效，是模型缺少微环境、药物暴露不匹配，还是临床终点定义错误？
  6. 个体化制造产品的效力、纯度或周转时间改善，是否被制造失败率、成本、可及性或监管限制抵消？
  7. 所谓不能为个体制造药物究竟是生物学限制、临床证据限制、制造限制，还是支付和监管限制？

  BianLun 每轮必须输出：

  round_summary:
    resolved_disputes: []
    unresolved_disputes: []
    causal_links_requiring_revision: []
    evidence_missing: []
    required_mingli_revision: []

  MingLi 必须保留修订前后版本、修改理由、引用变化和仍未解决的不确定性。

  ## 最终交付物与安全边界

  最终输出必须包括：

  - Objective Decomposition、每个 SH 的状态和组合结论门槛；
  - P0–P4 文献覆盖、子空间覆盖、来源质量和预印本缺口报告；
  - PaperGraph 因果证据图与断链位置；
  - TanXi ranked gaps 与 mechanism_draft；
  - Socrates evidence contract、反事实设计、第一性原理待补项；
  - 初始假设、3–5 轮 DuZhi 质询、BianLun 归纳、MingLi 修订轨迹；
  - YanZhen 初审与复审；
  - 最终状态：ACCEPT_FOR_EXPERIMENT、CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT、REJECTED 或 REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW；
  - 可证伪的验证设计，包括患者选择、对照、混杂控制、外部验证、统计分析计划和数据治理要求；
  - 个体化制造路径的 CMC 验证：身份链、关键质量属性、效力、纯度、无菌、批间一致性、周转时间、失败率和成本；
  - 未解决证据缺口、不能外推的人群/治疗范围，以及后续定向检索建议。

  所有涉及真实患者数据、治疗分配、剂量改变、细胞或基因治疗制造的实验，都只能作为研究设计输出；实际执行必须经过 IRB/伦理
  审查、临床试验注册、知情同意、隐私与数据治理审查，以及相应药品监管要求。

controls: matched controls for genetic background, organ function, and prior treatment history
sub_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If PK parameters, toxicity, or dosing do not vary with genetic variants and molecular phenotypes in a consistent and reproducible manner.

query_variants: genetic variants molecular phenotypes pharmacokinetics toxicity dosing

evidence_window.P0_latest_preprint: recent preprint evidence on genetic and molecular predictors of PK and dosing
evidence_window.P1_recent_primary: recent primary studies validating the predictive power of genetic and molecular markers on PK and dosing
evidence_window.P2_mechanism_or_theory: mechanistic or theoretical evidence linking genetic variants to PK parameters and dosing

alternative_mechanisms: Variation in PK due to environmental factors rather than genetic or molecular differences.
sub_hypotheses.source_objective: 在定义明确的患者群体、疾病类型和治疗类别中，个体的遗传变异、肿瘤或疾病分子表型、器官功能、免疫状态、既往治疗史以及制造过程参数，是否能够以可重复、可校准、因果上可解释的方式预测：1. 某一治疗方案的药代动力学暴露、毒性和适宜剂量；2. 某一靶向药物、免疫治疗、细胞治疗或核酸药物的临床获益与耐药风险；3. 个体化制剂或自体细胞产品能否在身份、纯度、效力、无菌性、批间一致性、周转时间和成本约束下稳定制造；4. 上述预测在不同祖源、年龄、肝肾功能、疾病分期、医疗中心和检测平台迁移时是否仍成立。禁止将统计相关、患者画像匹配或模型准确率直接解释为因果治疗获益；禁止将实验室样本、类器官、动物模型或单中心回顾性结果直接外推为临床个体化治疗有效；禁止将技术尚不成熟误判为生物学上绝对不可行。执行完整闭环：Boxue → Objective Decomposition → ZhiZhi → TanXi → Socrates → MingLi → YanZhen → DuZhi → BianLun → MingLi 修订 → YanZhen 复审。

evidence_window.P2_mechanism_or_theory: mechanistic or theoretical evidence on the robustness of predictive models to population and platform variations

alternative_mechanisms: Model performance is more influenced by data quality and sample size than by population and platform diversity.
sub_hypotheses.source_objective: 在定义明确的患者群体、疾病类型和治疗类别中，个体的遗传变异、肿瘤或疾病分子表型、器官功能、免疫状态、既往治疗史以及制造过程参数，是否能够以可重复、可校准、因果上可解释的方式预测：1. 某一治疗方案的药代动力学暴露、毒性和适宜剂量；2. 某一靶向药物、免疫治疗、细胞治疗或核酸药物的临床获益与耐药风险；3. 个体化制剂或自体细胞产品能否在身份、纯度、效力、无菌性、批间一致性、周转时间和成本约束下稳定制造；4. 上述预测在不同祖源、年龄、肝肾功能、疾病分期、医疗中心和检测平台迁移时是否仍成立。禁止将统计相关、患者画像匹配或模型准确率直接解释为因果治疗获益；禁止将实验室样本、类器官、动物模型或单中心回顾性结果直接外推为临床个体化治疗有效；禁止将技术尚不成熟误判为生物学上绝对不可行。执行完整闭环：Boxue → Objective Decomposition → ZhiZhi → TanXi → Socrates → MingLi → YanZhen → DuZhi → BianLun → MingLi 修订 → YanZhen 复审。

combination_hypothesis.statement: If SH1, SH2, SH3, and SH4 are all supported, then it can be concluded that individualized medicine can be designed and manufactured in a way that is predictable, reproducible, and causally interpretable across different patient subgroups and therapeutic contexts.
combination_hypothesis.integration_test: A comprehensive test that integrates the findings from each sub-hypothesis to evaluate the overall effectiveness and reliability of personalized medicine approaches.

retrieval: Use semantic_scholar, PubMed, and arXiv. Clinical preprints can supplement medRxiv/bioRxiv but must be marked as unreviewed. Import at least 15 quality-screened papers. Do not fabricate papers, clinical trials, regulatory documents, effect sizes, patient outcomes, or guideline recommendations. Ensure coverage of each selected sub-hypothesis's evidence window. Exclude or de-prioritize content that only reports model AUC without clinical endpoints, only reports disease associations without treatment strategies, is unrelated to precision medicine, or claims direct patient benefits from animal or cell line studies.
gap_detection: Prioritize gaps where there is a lack of measurable intermediates, intervention evidence, or external validation. Focus on whether certain biomarkers predict treatment effects or merely reflect prognosis, baseline multi-omics predictions and clonal evolution, inconsistencies between in vitro and in vivo responses, unverified causal chains in personalized cell/nucleic acid products, calibration failures across different ancestries, ages, and medical centers, and whether improvements in prediction performance are offset by manufacturing cycle, failure rates, costs, and accessibility. Do not list gaps based on the absence of a method in a particular scenario.
hypothesis: Generate hypotheses that include a clear patient scope, treatment scope, and observable endpoints. Each hypothesis must contain a causal chain, operating conditions, observables, null hypothesis, alternative explanations, falsification condition, and source mapping. Avoid generic templates and ensure each step is supported by Socrates evidence contracts.
verification: Perform initial and final audits for internal, data, and causal consistency. Conduct at least two regime-shift tests to evaluate the robustness of the hypotheses under different conditions. If the initial audit is not passed, provide specific feedback to MingLi and DuZhi. Final outputs must include Objective Decomposition, literature coverage, causal evidence graph, ranked gaps, evidence contracts, initial and revised hypotheses, debate summaries, and final status. All experiments involving real patient data, treatment allocation, dose changes, or cell/gene therapy manufacturing must undergo IRB/ethics review, clinical trial registration, informed consent, privacy and data governance review, and appropriate regulatory approval.

decomposition_rules: Each sub-hypothesis must expose an intervention, observable outcome, falsification condition, and evidence window.
A combined conclusion is admissible only after the relevant component hypotheses are independently evaluated.
Missing quantitative bounds remain explicitly unresolved; they must not be fabricated from the objective.

steps.evidence: Median OS was significantly longer in patients treated with TMZ+IRI compared to controls (25 vs. 8 months, p = 0.002).

steps.claim: One-year OS rates were 58.2% for the TMZ+IRI group and 25.4% for the control group.

chains.outcome: Significant overall survival benefit of TMZ+IRI over other second-line regimens in relapsed SCLC.

chains.trigger: Lack of external validation and methodological heterogeneity

steps.claim: Heterogeneity in ML tasks, reporting formats, and effect measures complicates data comparability.
steps.evidence: Significant heterogeneity was noted in ML tasks, reporting formats, and effect measures across studies, highlighting a lack of standardised reporting and challenges in data comparability.

steps.claim: Lack of external validation limits the clinical applicability of ML models.
steps.evidence: Only five studies (25%) performed external validation. The clinical applicability of these ML models remains limited by the lack of external validation and methodological heterogeneity.

chains.outcome: Challenges in translating ML from a promising research tool into a reliable clinical practice component.
observables: Lack of uniform reporting of effect measures
Small sample sizes in some studies
interventions: Standardised reporting and rigorous external validation

chains.trigger: Development and validation of machine learning models

steps.claim: Models trained on 207 patients from MGH and validated on 143 patients from MDACC
steps.evidence: The models were trained on 207 patients from Massachusetts General Hospital. Performance was quantified using Harrell's c-index, area under the curve (AUC), and accuracy in high-risk populations. Models' structures were optimized in a nested cross-validation approach to prevent overfitting. A study analysis plan was registered before external validation using 143 patients from MD Anderson Cancer Center.

steps.claim: Survival model stratified high-risk versus low-risk patients well in the external validation cohort (c-index = 0.75)
steps.evidence: The survival model stratified high-risk versus low-risk patients well in the external validation cohort (c-index = 0.75), better than existing risk scores.

steps.claim: Predictions of 1-year survival and nonlocal failure were excellent (external AUC = 0.74 and 0.80, respectively), especially in the high-risk group (accuracy > 90%)
steps.evidence: Predictions of 1-year survival and nonlocal failure were excellent (external AUC = 0.74 and 0.80, respectively), especially in the high-risk group (accuracy > 90%).

chains.outcome: Reliable outcome predictions and novel strategies for patient stratification and treatment selection
observables: c-index, AUC, and accuracy in high-risk populations
interventions: Machine learning models developed using linear and nonlinear algorithms
External validation on an independent cohort

evidence_type_annotations.claim: PCR-ARMS and PCR-RFLP genotyping, plasma MTX concentration measurement
MTHFR C677T, DHFR19 Ins/del, GGH −401 C > T, and MTR A2756G polymorphisms affect MTX toxicity and pharmacokinetics in Egyptian patients with ALL or NHL
MTHFR polymorphism played the main role in MTX toxicities.
The pharmacokinetics of MTX was affected by MTR polymorphism. GGH mutation was mainly concerned with anaemia. Pharmacogenetic testing are recommended to optimise MTX therapy.
Plasma concentrations of MTX were measured after 72 h of initiation of infusion.

references: Leslie Carol Cerpa-Castro et al. (2025) Genetic variants and clinical factors affecting the response to 5-fluorouracil–based treatment in Chilean patients with advanced colorectal cancer. doi:10.1200/jco.2025.43.4_suppl.114

evidence_type_annotations.claim: retrospective nested case-control study
identification of genetic variants and development of multivariate models to predict chemotherapy safety
the study is an initial step and more work is needed to develop comprehensive predictive models

This study represents an initial step toward developing predictive models for ADRs related to 5-FU, such as neuropathy, mucositis, and hematological and skin toxicities. In the future, these findings may contribute to pharmacogenetic-based dose adjustment models aimed at reducing ADRs in Chilean CRC...[truncated]
Late diagnosis results in approximately 25% of patients presenting with metastatic disease, with a five-year survival rate of about 14%.
In the future, these findings may contribute to pharmacogenetic-based dose adjustment models aimed at reducing ADRs in Chilean CRC patients undergoing 5-FU-based treatment.

Comprehensive genomic and transcriptomic profiling could reliably unveil genetic details not provided by DNA-only CGP. The integration of ctDNA detection further helped detect tumor-agnostic mutations and monitor treatment response.
BackgroundComprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) is a time- and tissue-efficient method to help guide precision oncology.
MethodsGenomic DNA and mRNA simultaneously extracted from 604 archived tissue samples of 12 cancer types were used.

mRNA transcriptome profiling was performed to further capture fusion variants, and to predict tissue of origin (TOO) using our ensemble model OriCUP, an algorithm trained on 9,889 samples and independently validated on 731 samples.
In a cohort of 55 metastatic lung cancer patients, longitudinal plasma ctDNA was analyzed using a hybrid tumor-informed and tumor-agnostic approach to predict progression-free survival (PFS).
ResultsAmong all biomarkers, DNA-sequencing using HDP achieved higher sensitivity than the standard panel design to identify copy number variations at chromosome-, gene- and exon-levels.

The detection rate of fusion variants using DNA-sequencing alone was 20% lower than mRNA-sequencing in reference samples, while combination of both methods was essential to maximize fusion detection in clinical FFPE samples.
For TOO, our OriCup model achieved prediction accuracy of 87.7% for primary tumors and 81.4% for metastatic tumors.
ConclusionsComprehensive genomic and transcriptomic profiling could reliably unveil genetic details not provided by DNA-only CGP.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: machine learning models (linear and nonlinear algorithms)

Machine learning approaches can provide reliable outcome predictions in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma after RT in diverse cohorts across institutions. The excellent performance, particularly in high-risk patients, suggests novel strategies for patient stratification and treatment selection....[truncated]

MATERIALS AND METHODS The models were developed using linear and nonlinear algorithms, predicting survival, nonlocal failure, radiation-induced liver disease, and lymphopenia from baseline patient and treatment parameters.
Performance was quantified using Harrell's c-index, area under the curve (AUC), and accuracy in high-risk populations.
Models' structures were optimized in a nested cross-validation approach to prevent overfitting.
RESULTS The survival model stratified high-risk versus low-risk patients well in the external validation cohort (c-index = 0.75), better than existing risk scores.
Predictions of 1-year survival and nonlocal failure were excellent (external AUC = 0.74 and 0.80, respectively), especially in the high-risk group (accuracy > 90%).

Cause-of-death analysis showed differential modes of treatment failure in these cohorts and indicated that these models could be used to stratify RT patients for liver-sparing treatment regimen or combination approaches with systemic agents.

Predictions of liver disease and lymphopenia were good but less robust (external AUC = 0.68 and 0.7, respectively), suggesting the need for more comprehensive consideration of dosimetry and better predictive biomarkers.
The liver disease model showed excellent accuracy in the high-risk group (92%) and revealed possible interactions of platelet count with initial liver function.

top_hypotheses.statement: If the competing claims about decentralized manufacturing of cell and gene therapy products are evaluated under matched the experimental, observational, or simulation conditions that differ between the claims conditions, then AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic will separate which mechanism holds and identify the boundary condition the condition where the competing explanations diverge. The decisive test is constrained to AUC∞, Cmax, t1/2, adverse drug reaction (ADR) occurrence under an explicit stress regime.
top_hypotheses.mechanism: Concrete mechanism chain: (1) method capability: 16S (V3-V4) rRNA sequencing, Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazard models contributes through testing mechanistic predictions under controlled parameter variations; (2) scenario target: in decentralized manufacturing of cell and gene therapy products, the affected process is a measurable biological or clinical mechanism such as expression, pathway activation, response, adverse effect, or persistence; (3) measurable consequence: the bridge must produce a preregistered change in AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic. The method's input requirements are broad or not clearly specified; the experiment must make them explicit. The required bridge is multi-omics. The decisive prediction is that this concrete bridge, not a generic representation change, will alter AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic; if the bridge data or causal link is absent, the hypothesis should fail rather than be reinterpreted post hoc.
top_hypotheses.expected_value: Contradiction gaps are high-value because resolving them can update mechanism understanding, not merely fill a sparse method-scenario cell.
top_hypotheses.test_plan: Build a minimal benchmark for decentralized manufacturing of cell and gene therapy products; compare 16S (V3-V4) rRNA sequencing, Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazard models against canonical baselines; measure AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic; include negative controls, ablations, and failure-mode analysis. Add a preregistered stress test for AUC∞, Cmax, t1/2, adverse drug reaction (ADR) occurrence.

counterfactual_experiments.predicted_outcome_if_mechanism_false: AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic does not change systematically when the experimental, observational, or simulation conditions that differ between the claims is varied, or it changes without the predicted mediator response.

top_hypotheses.statement: If 16S (V3-V4) rRNA sequencing, Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazard models is used to perturb or stratify genetic variants and molecular phenotypes in Acute lymphoblastic leukaemia (ALL) or Non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) in Egyptian adult patients under explicit failure-mode stress tests, then pharmacokinetic exposure, toxicity levels, optimal dosing will show a directional or non-monotonic boundary at a preregistered stress threshold rather than an open-ended improvement claim.
top_hypotheses.mechanism: Concrete mechanism chain: (1) method capability: 16S (V3-V4) rRNA sequencing, Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazard models contributes through testing mechanistic predictions under controlled parameter variations; (2) scenario target: in Acute lymphoblastic leukaemia (ALL) or Non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) in Egyptian adult patients, the affected process is a measurable biological or clinical mechanism such as expression, pathway activation, response, adverse effect, or persistence; (3) measurable consequence: the bridge must produce a preregistered change in AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic. The method's input requirements are broad or not clearly specified; the experiment must make them explicit. The required bridge is multi-omics. The decisive prediction is that this concrete bridge, not a generic representation change, will alter AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic; if the bridge data or causal link is absent, the hypothesis should fail rather than be reinterpreted post hoc.
top_hypotheses.expected_value: A broken causal link is a direct, falsifiable research opening rather than a sparse method-scenario combination.
top_hypotheses.test_plan: Build a minimal benchmark for Acute lymphoblastic leukaemia (ALL) or Non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) in Egyptian adult patients; compare 16S (V3-V4) rRNA sequencing, Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazard models against canonical baselines; measure AUC scores, sensitivity, specificity, Dice scores, K-statistic; include negative controls, ablations, and failure-mode analysis.
